title: Scolymocephalus angustatus
taxonomy: Scolymocephalus angustatus is a plant species within the family of flowering plants known as Magnoliophyta, and more specifically within the clade Angiosperms. It is a member of the order Magnoliales, and is closely related to other flowering plant families such as Annonaceae and Myristicaceae. The plant's classification also places it within the clade Superrosidae, a grouping of flowering plants that includes several orders, including Magnoliales and Laurales. At the level of the kingdom, Scolymocephalus angustatus is classified as a member of the kingdom Plantae. The species' classification within the plant kingdom is further defined by its relationships to other flowering plants.

If you are looking for information on Scolymus hispidus:

Scolymus hispidus, also known as the Milk Thistle or Scolymus, is a plant species in the sunflower family that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties.

The health benefits of Scolymus hispidus include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and hepatoprotective properties, which can help protect the liver from damage and improve overall health.

The bioactive constituents of Scolymus hispidus include flavonoids, saponins, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Scolymus hispidus include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including liver and digestive issues.
